calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Sancimino for Gregory Lind.

The Court stated that the issues are $2,400.00 of personal property that should've been, but were not inventoried, the preliminary distribution that was made prior to receiving 13101 documents and fees. Ms. Sancimino stated that several items (pots, pans, odds and ends) were sold post death for $2400.00, that she has copies of three checks and that they were accounted for in the receipts. Ms. Sancimino also stated that distribution was made to the Mattingly Trust, that it wasn't until after that the Court requested the 13101 document, that her Client couldn't back date it and that it won't happen again. In addition, Ms. Sancimino stated that the majority of fees were with the will contest and that they were required to establish special administration. The First and Final Report of Executor on Waiver of Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Petition For Preliminary Distribution, For Allowance Of Payment In Fulfillment Of Settlement Agreement, And Allowance Of Compensation To Executor And To Attorneys
Off Calendar Mar-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ritzi Lam for and with Gregory Lind (Executor).
Cyrus Frank (Claimant) in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the 60-day period for the Franchise Tax Board ("FTB") hasn't run and stated that it is inclined to grant the petition allowing payment, but not before March 7th subject to the FTB not having put in a significant claim. Ms. Lam stated that they were looking for distribution as of today, that the FTB received faxed notice over 60-days ago, that her Client is not aware of any State liability and that distribution is under $450,000.00 (less than half of the Estate). The Court granted the Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc. (subject to receipt and review of the order), but distribution will wait until March 7, 2017. Ms. Lam is to provide a revised order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Authorizing And Approving Special Administrators Compromise And Settlement Of Claims
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Continued To Oct-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Continued To Oct-04-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Rosenberg for petitioner Gregory Lind; Cyrus Frank, contestant in pro per.

Ms. Rosenberg stated that she was happy to inform the Court that a settlement had been reached, and that she had filed a petition to approve it yesterday. She did not yet have a file-endorsed copy or hearing date. She requested a continuance of the hearing and extension of the letters until the new hearing date. The Court continued he matter to October 4, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, and agreed to extend the letters to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Continued To Aug-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Sancimino for (petitioner/respondent) Gregory Lind. Cyrus Frank (contestant) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the parties are continuing to work through mediation and have another upcoming session, that it wants to make sure that the continuance includes the extension of the special letters to that date and confirmed that there was no objection to the extension of the letters. Mr. Frank asked what would be discussed at the next hearing. The Court stated that it hopes the matter will have settled. Ms. Sancimino stated that a proposed order had been submitted regarding the status on mediation. The Court stated that it has not seen it yet and is not sure that it is appropriate. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to August 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Continued To Jul-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Sancimino for and with (petitioner) Gregory Lind. Cyrus Frank (contestant) in Propria Persona.

The Court asked the parties where they are and if they are still mediating. Mr. Frank stated that on February 9, 2016 he stated that he was amenable to mediation, that Ms. Sancimino said she would provide names of 3 mediators, that he was then sent 30 pages of discovery and that he thought that they would mediate and try to settle. Ms. Sancimino stated that prior to the hearing Mr. Frank said that he wanted to take depositions, that they needed to do 2 ex parte hearings to get the special administrator appointed and that Mr. Frank objected, that they submitted discovery requests to Mr. Frank, that the real property has been appraised by the probate referee and that the special letters of administration expire today and they would like to get them extended. The Court asked if Mr. Frank had any objections to the extension of the letters and he responded no, the court will grant the extension to the next hearing date on the same original terms, confirmed that Mr. Frank had said that he needed to take depositions and asked if the parties still intend to mediate. Mr. Frank stated that he would like to take Mr. Lind's deposition on May 12, 2016, that he has spoken to a court reporter but that he thinks Mr. Lind is out of town on that date. The Court stated that Mr. Frank had made it a point that discovery had to happen before the mediation and could have sent out a notice the next day. Ms. Sancimino stated that they need a 3 month continuance and that they still have not received anything from Mr. Frank. The Court stated that we are talking about taking depositions which is exactly where we were 3 months ago, instructed the parties to send out their notices and get it done, that it will continue the petition for the parties to finish pre-mediation discovery and participate in mediation and that it is not difficult to get depositions and mediation set up. The petition for probate of will and letters testamentary was continued to July 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar Feb-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Continued To Feb-26-16 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 202

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Continued To Apr-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Sancimino for and with (petitioner) Gregory Lind. Cyrus Frank in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we now have objections and a will contest and asked if the parties have had a chance to speak to each other. Ms. Sancimino stated that they have not spoken, that they just received the opposition yesterday and that the will contest was not properly served. Mr. Frank stated that he is trying to serve 14 people plus institutions, clarified that he has spoken with counsel twice, that he sent a letter asking for information but that there is no cooperation. The Court asked how they plan to resolve this, confirmed that the parties were agreeable to mediation and that this matter does not qualify for Pro Bono mediation. Mr. Frank stated that he needs to take 2 depositions. The Court stated that it will give the parties 3 months to do discovery and mediation. Ms. Sancimino asked if her client could be appointed in the interim. The Court stated that he could not, that there is a will contest, that Mr. Frank is objecting, that they could apply for special letters and told the parties to talk to each other and see if they can reach an agreement. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to April 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Feb-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Sancimino for (petitioner) Gregory Lind. Cyrus Frank in Propria Persona.

This petition had been granted without hearing.
Mr. Frank stated that he has a will contest and objections but that he was unable to get them filed. The Court stated that it will need to continue the matter for Mr. Frank to be able to file his documents and for the petitioner to have an opportunity to respond. Ms. Sancimino suggested that a special administrator be appointed in the interim. The court stated that that request is not before it and that she will need to follow the proper procedure. Mr. Frank stated that he needs guidance in regards to the pleadings he would like to file. The Court stated that it does not know the substance of the pleadings and is not able to give advice. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to February 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
